posts.title: Sprecher East Is Coming Together
posts.author: Amine Boulaajaj
posts.excerpt: One person, a bunch of AI agents, and a dream to build real community on Madison's Far East Side. Here's why Sprecher East exists — and why we need you.
posts.content:
Hey neighbor.

I'm Amine, and I live right here in the Sprecher East area on Madison's Far East Side. You probably know the feeling — you wave at the same people walking their dogs, you see the same faces at the park, but somehow we never really connect. We drive past each other on Cottage Grove Road, we all complain about the same potholes, and we all wish someone would do... something.

Well, this is that something.

Sprecher East is a grassroots neighborhood initiative. No corporate backing, no government mandate, no HOA board sending you angry letters about your fence height. Just one neighbor who decided to stop waiting and start building.

Here's the honest truth: right now, this is mostly me and a team of AI tools. Yes, AI. I use AI agents to help research, write, build this website, and keep things organized. I'm completely transparent about that because I believe trust is built on honesty, not appearances. Every piece of content, every decision, every outreach goes through a real human — me — before it goes live.

But here's the thing: this can't just be me. A neighborhood isn't one person with a laptop. It's all of us — the families in Meadowlands, the folks in Door Creek, the new residents in Reston Heights. It's the parents at Elvehjem Elementary, the runners on the Door Creek bike path, the people who know the best pizza delivery that actually comes to our side of town.

What we're building:

- A real neighborhood association that advocates for our area at City Hall
- A website with events, news, and resources that actually matter to us
- Community connections that go deeper than a Nextdoor post
- A voice for District 16 residents who feel like the far east side gets forgotten

What we need from you:

- Show up. Come to a meeting, say hi, bring a friend.
- Speak up. Got a neighborhood concern? An idea? A complaint? We want to hear it.
- Spread the word. Tell your neighbors about sprechereast.com.
- Volunteer. Even an hour a month makes a real difference.

We're not official yet — registration with the City of Madison takes time. But the community we're building? That's already real. And it starts with you.

Let's do this together.

— Amine Boulaajaj
Founder, Sprecher East
sprechereast@gmail.com

posts.title: WisDOT Holds Public Hearings on I-39/90/94 Corridor Study
posts.author: Sprecher East
posts.excerpt: The Wisconsin Department of Transportation is holding public hearings to gather community feedback on major corridor improvements to I-39/90/94. Residents are encouraged to participate — in person or virtually.
posts.content:
The Wisconsin Department of Transportation (WisDOT) held three public hearings to update the community on the I-39/90/94 Corridor Study's Draft Environmental Impact Statement and preferred alternatives.

A virtual hearing took place July 29, 2024 via YouTube Live. An in-person hearing was held July 30, 2024 at Madison College Truax Building, 1701 Wright Street, Madison, WI 53704.

For questions or to provide testimony, contact David Schmidt, WisDOT Project Manager at (608) 246-3867 or David2Schmidt@dot.wi.gov.

More information is available at the WisDOT project page.

posts.title: Welcome to the Sprecher East Website
posts.author: Sprecher East
posts.excerpt: We've refreshed our neighborhood website to make it easier to find events, news, and resources. Sprecher East has been a community since 2006 — and we're excited to keep growing together.
posts.content:
Sprecher East is one of the newest neighborhoods on the far east side of Madison. We are bounded by I-94 to the north, Door Creek Park to the east, Sprecher Road to the west, and Cottage Grove Road to the south.

Our neighborhood includes the Meadowlands, Door Creek, and Reston Heights subdivisions. The Sprecher East Neighborhood Association has served this community since 2006.

Membership is open to any resident within our boundaries, and it's always free and optional. We're here to represent neighborhood interests, keep everyone informed, and build a great community together.

Stay connected — check this site for upcoming events and news!
